text:
A deflated Eric Donovan joins Mikey Stafford to discuss a mixed day in the ring for Ireland.

Kellie Harrington's history-making guaranteed bronze was sandwiched between defeats for Jude Gallagher and Aoife O'Rourke.

O'Rourke's defeat was a bit of a shocker but Eric says, while the referee was a little weak, Aoife will be hugely disappointed with how she allowed her Polish opponent, Elzbieta Wojcik, to dictate terms.

Classy Harrington displayed a very different way to win and Eric believes the back-to-back Olympic medallist ranks among ireland's best ever amateur boxers.

Earlier Mikey chatted to Gary Moran ahead of the start of the men's golf tournament, where Shane Lowry and Rory McIlroy must be considered medal hopes.

But, the question hasn't quite gone away, does golf belong in the Olympics?

title: Olympics Podcast: Cruel defeat and the sweet science
description: A deflated Eric Donovan joins Mikey Stafford to discuss a mixed day in the ring for Ireland, while Gary Moran previews the men's golf tournament, where Shane Lowry and Rory McIlroy must be considered medal hopes
